Rescopes `ROADMAP.md` so the next release gate is Agent OS Stability & Self-Healing instead of the deferred harness PoC. The roadmap now names Epic #14039 and milestone #8 as the live scope map, preserves the harness as v13.2, and makes the deferred set explicit.

Evidence: L1 local static/docs checks plus live GitHub scope verification -> L1 required for this roadmap-only close target. Residual: none for #14038.

## Deltas from ticket
- Added Epic #14039 as the relationship-graph authority for v13.1 scope.
- Linked #13999 and #14027 under #14039, and updated milestone #8 open scope to the Agent OS stability set.
- Moved the old harness/product items out of open v13.1 scope and explicitly deferred #10291 with the H4 cloud/self-defense horizon.

### 🔬 Depth Floor

**Challenge OR documented search (per guide §7.1):**

- **Challenge (non-blocking decomposition gap):** the gate names "the Sandman handoff substrate is again trustworthy," and the **Restore the planning substrate** cornerstone cites #12065 + #14030 — but V-B-A shows **neither covers restoring the corruption-degraded `sandman_handoff`**: #12065 is the broader `[Epic] Orchestrator-as-SSOT for the REM (Sandman) Pipeline`, and #14030 is backup reliability/restorability. No open sub explicitly scopes "repair/regenerate the degraded `sandman_handoff` to a trustworthy state." So a fresh-context peer reading this gate cannot find the ticket that delivers it — the exact incomplete-epic-scope risk the re-scope itself is meant to fix. Follow-up: file a sub under #14039 explicitly scoping the handoff restoration (or, if it's an implicit side-effect of the #13999 MC recovery, say so on the cornerstone). I'll file it.

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit (per guide §7.4):** Pass. The thesis-change prose ("a green harness can still be data-gutted") matches the #13999 reality; the gate is an honest single bar; the cornerstone done-signals are concrete + map to live tickets; "deferred intact, not deleted" is substantiated by the full harness ticket list moved to the v13.2 section.
